managed_surface_reconciliation_failed.title: Magic Areas could not reconcile a managed Home Assistant helper
managed_surface_reconciliation_failed.description:
Magic Areas could not {action} the managed `{domain}` helper `{surface}`. Error: `{error}`.

The affected helper may be missing, stale, or out of sync until the underlying problem is fixed and Magic Areas reconciles it again.

user.title: Create a Magic Area
user.description: Choose the area you would like to make it magic!

abort.invalid_area: The selected area does not exist
abort.no_more_areas: All your areas are already magic!

abort.entry_not_loaded: Magic Areas is still loading or is not currently loaded. Reload the integration and try again.

select_features.title: Feature selection
select_features.description: Select the features you want to enable for this area. Features with settings add a configuration menu after submit; helper-only features are enabled with sensible defaults without extra menu pages.

data.wasp_in_a_box: Create a Wasp in a Box sensor

data_description.fan_groups: Creates a native fan helper group and adds a configuration menu for trigger state and setpoint behavior.
data_description.climate_control: Adds a configuration menu for choosing the climate entity and presets used for room states.
data_description.media_player_groups: Creates a native media-player helper group using default grouping and does not add a configuration menu.
data_description.light_groups: Creates native light helper groups and adds a configuration menu for roles, brightness behavior, and Adaptive Lighting coordination.
data_description.cover_groups: Creates native cover helper groups by device class using default grouping and does not add a configuration menu.
data_description.area_aware_media_player: Adds a configuration menu for notification targets and states used by the area-aware media player.
data_description.aggregates: Creates native aggregate helpers and adds a configuration menu for device classes, thresholds, and minimum entity counts.
data_description.health: Creates a native health helper and adds a configuration menu for monitored binary-sensor device classes.
data_description.presence_hold: Creates a presence hold switch and adds a configuration menu for the hold timeout.
data_description.ble_trackers: Creates a BLE tracker monitor and adds a configuration menu for the tracker entities to watch.
data_description.wasp_in_a_box: Creates a Wasp in a Box sensor and adds a configuration menu for delay, timeout, and sensor classes.

show_menu.title: Magic Areas Options
show_menu.description: Choose what to configure for this area. Changes are staged inside this flow and are not saved until you select `Save & Exit`.

feature_conf_health.title: Health sensors
feature_conf_health.description: Choose what to configure for health monitoring in this area.

feature_conf_health_settings.title: Health sensor settings
feature_conf_health_settings.description: Choose the binary sensor device classes tracked by the health sensor for this area.

feature_conf_light_groups.title: Light Groups
feature_conf_light_groups.description: Magic Areas creates these four (plus a global one) [light groups](https://github.com/jseidl/hass-magic_areas/wiki/Light-Groups) (if you assign lights to them) for you to use. These by default are plain light groups but can be automatically controlled if you assign them to area states and enable the light control switch (that will be created when you enable this feature). All lights will be turned off when an area is cleared. Additional states can be unlocked by configuring `Advanced area state tracking`.

data.overhead_lights_states: States which overhead lights are turned on
data.overhead_lights_act_on: When should overhead lights be controlled?
data.sleep_lights_states: States which sleep lights are turned on
data.sleep_lights_act_on: When should sleep lights be controlled?
data.accent_lights_states: States which accent lights are turned on
data.accent_lights_act_on: When should accent lights be controlled?
data.task_lights_states: States which task lights are turned on
data.task_lights_act_on: When should task lights be controlled?

data_description.brightness_mode: Select how `bright` affects automation for this group: Classic keeps legacy behavior (bright can block turn-on and can trigger turn-off), Advisory ignores bright for turn-off decisions, and Adaptive allows bright-driven turn-off only when safeguards pass.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_mode: Select whether Magic Areas ignores Adaptive Lighting, adopts existing Adaptive Lighting switch sets, or manages Adaptive Lighting configs for selected light roles.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_manage_all_lights: When enabled, Magic Areas creates a room-level Adaptive Lighting config containing all lights in the area. The all-lights switch can then coordinate that room-level Adaptive Lighting group separately from role groups.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_managed_roles: Select which Magic Areas light roles should receive Magic Areas-managed Adaptive Lighting configs. Adaptive Lighting still owns brightness/color/sleep tuning.
data_description.bright_min_on_seconds: In `adaptive` mode, require lights to remain on for at least this many seconds before bright-driven turn-off is allowed.
data_description.bright_dwell_seconds: In `adaptive` mode, require the area to remain `bright` for at least this many seconds before bright-driven turn-off is allowed.
data_description.bright_attribution_hold_seconds: In `adaptive` mode, suppress bright-driven turn-off for this many seconds after Magic Areas issues a light-group action or a configured room light increases output.
data_description.adaptive_require_ambient_rise: When enabled, `adaptive` mode requires in-room lux to show a meaningful rise within a rolling window before bright-driven turn-off is allowed.
data_description.ambient_rise_window_seconds: Rolling time window (seconds) used to evaluate in-room ambient rise.
data_description.ambient_rise_min_delta: Minimum in-room lux increase required within the ambient-rise window when ambient rise is required.
data_description.inside_bright_entity: Optional binary bright/dark entity for the room (recommended: the Magic Areas threshold-light binary sensor). This is not a lux sensor. In `advisory`/`adaptive`, this overrides inferred room-bright behavior for bright gating.
data_description.outside_context_source: Outside context used by `adaptive` mode for bright-driven turn-off eligibility.
data_description.outside_bright_entity: Optional binary entity that represents whether it is bright outside. When set, this is used as the first outside-context gate before sun/lux checks.
data_description.outside_lux_entity: Optional outdoor illuminance sensor used when outside context source is `outside_lux`.
data_description.outside_lux_min: Minimum outside lux required for adaptive bright-driven turn-off when source is `outside_lux`.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_entity: Optional in-room illuminance sensor used for outside/inside contrast checks when source is `outside_lux`.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_delta: Optional required lux delta (`outside - inside`) before adaptive bright-driven turn-off is allowed. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_ratio_min_percent: Optional required outside/inside lux ratio percentage (100 = 1.0x) for adaptive bright-driven turn-off when source is `outside_lux`. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.overhead_lights_states: Configure which area states will trigger this light group. Light groups will be turned `on` while area is on this state and `off` if the area loses these states. For example, if you want the light to stay `on` after an extended period of time, you need to select the `extended` state too.
data_description.overhead_lights_act_on: Select if this light group should be controlled only when an area first gets occupied, only on area state changes or both.
data_description.sleep_lights_states: Configure which area states will trigger this light group. Light groups will be turned `on` while area is on this state and `off` if the area loses these states. For example, if you want the light to stay `on` after an extended period of time, you need to select the `extended` state too.
data_description.sleep_lights_act_on: Select if this light group should be controlled only when an area first gets occupied, only on area state changes or both.
data_description.accent_lights_states: Configure which area states will trigger this light group. Light groups will be turned `on` while area is on this state and `off` if the area loses these states. For example, if you want the light to stay `on` after an extended period of time, you need to select the `extended` state too.
data_description.accent_lights_act_on: Select if this light group should be controlled only when an area first gets occupied, only on area state changes or both.
data_description.task_lights_states: Configure which area states will trigger this light group. Light groups will be turned `on` while area is on this state and `off` if the area loses these states. For example, if you want the light to stay `on` after an extended period of time, you need to select the `extended` state too.
data_description.task_lights_act_on: Select if this light group should be controlled only when an area first gets occupied, only on area state changes or both.

feature_conf_light_groups_roles.title: Light roles
feature_conf_light_groups_roles.description: Assign lights to room roles and choose which area states and events control each role.

data.sleep_lights_states: States which sleep lights are turned on

feature_conf_light_groups_brightness.title: Light brightness behavior
feature_conf_light_groups_brightness.description: Choose a brightness behavior mode first. Mode-specific settings are shown on the next screen.

data_description.brightness_mode: Select how `bright` affects automation for this group: Classic keeps legacy behavior (bright can block turn-on and can trigger turn-off), Advisory ignores bright for turn-off decisions, and Adaptive allows bright-driven turn-off only when safeguards pass.

feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_adv.title: Advisory brightness settings
feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_adv.description: Configure optional binary bright/dark hints used by advisory behavior.

data_description.inside_bright_entity: Optional binary bright/dark entity for the room (recommended: the Magic Areas threshold-light binary sensor). This is not a lux sensor. In `advisory`/`adaptive`, this overrides inferred room-bright behavior for bright gating.
data_description.outside_bright_entity: Optional binary entity that represents whether it is bright outside. When set, this is used as the first outside-context gate before sun/lux checks.

feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_ada.title: Adaptive brightness settings
feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_ada.description: Configure binary and lux safeguards for adaptive bright-driven turn-off.

data_description.bright_min_on_seconds: In `adaptive` mode, require lights to remain on for at least this many seconds before bright-driven turn-off is allowed.
data_description.bright_dwell_seconds: In `adaptive` mode, require the area to remain `bright` for at least this many seconds before bright-driven turn-off is allowed.
data_description.bright_attribution_hold_seconds: In `adaptive` mode, suppress bright-driven turn-off for this many seconds after Magic Areas issues a light-group action or a configured room light increases output.
data_description.adaptive_require_ambient_rise: When enabled, `adaptive` mode requires in-room lux to show a meaningful rise within a rolling window before bright-driven turn-off is allowed.
data_description.ambient_rise_window_seconds: Rolling time window (seconds) used to evaluate in-room ambient rise.
data_description.ambient_rise_min_delta: Minimum in-room lux increase required within the ambient-rise window when ambient rise is required.
data_description.inside_bright_entity: Optional binary bright/dark entity for the room (recommended: the Magic Areas threshold-light binary sensor). This is not a lux sensor. In `advisory`/`adaptive`, this overrides inferred room-bright behavior for bright gating.
data_description.outside_context_source: Outside context used by `adaptive` mode for bright-driven turn-off eligibility.
data_description.outside_bright_entity: Optional binary entity that represents whether it is bright outside. When set, this is used as the first outside-context gate before sun/lux checks.
data_description.outside_lux_entity: Optional outdoor illuminance sensor used when outside context source is `outside_lux`.
data_description.outside_lux_min: Minimum outside lux required for adaptive bright-driven turn-off when source is `outside_lux`.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_entity: Optional in-room illuminance sensor used for outside/inside contrast checks when source is `outside_lux`.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_delta: Optional required lux delta (`outside - inside`) before adaptive bright-driven turn-off is allowed. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_ratio_min_percent: Optional required outside/inside lux ratio percentage (100 = 1.0x) for adaptive bright-driven turn-off when source is `outside_lux`. Set to 0 to disable.

feature_conf_light_groups_adaptive_light.title: Adaptive Lighting coordination
feature_conf_light_groups_adaptive_light.description: Choose whether Magic Areas ignores Adaptive Lighting, links to existing Adaptive Lighting switch sets, or creates managed Adaptive Lighting configs for selected light roles.

data_description.adaptive_lighting_mode: Select whether Magic Areas ignores Adaptive Lighting, adopts existing Adaptive Lighting switch sets, or manages Adaptive Lighting configs for selected light roles.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_manage_all_lights: When enabled, Magic Areas creates a room-level Adaptive Lighting config containing all lights in the area. The all-lights switch can then coordinate that room-level Adaptive Lighting group separately from role groups.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_managed_roles: Select which Magic Areas light roles should receive Magic Areas-managed Adaptive Lighting configs. Adaptive Lighting still owns brightness/color/sleep tuning.

feature_conf_presence_hold.title: Presence hold
feature_conf_presence_hold.description: Choose what to configure for the presence hold switch in this area.

feature_conf_presence_hold_settings.title: Presence hold settings
feature_conf_presence_hold_settings.description: Configure how long the presence hold switch can keep the area occupied before it automatically turns off.

data.presence_hold_timeout: Time after the switch will be automatically turned `off`. (0 = timeout disabled).

feature_conf_area_aware_media_player.title: Area-aware media player
feature_conf_area_aware_media_player.description: Choose what to configure for area-aware media routing in this area.

feature_conf_area_aware_media_player_set.title: Area-aware media settings
feature_conf_area_aware_media_player_set.description: Configure broadcast targets and states for area-aware media notifications.

data.notification_devices: Media Player devices used for broadcasting
data.notification_states: Play notifications devices only when on these states

data_description.notification_states: Notifications will only be played when an area is on this state. Using `extended` will ensure you don't get notified in rooms you just passed by and not selecting `sleep` will prevent notifications while on this mode.

feature_conf_aggregates.title: Aggregate sensors
feature_conf_aggregates.description: Choose what to configure for aggregate helpers in this area.

feature_conf_aggregates_settings.title: Aggregate sensor settings
feature_conf_aggregates_settings.description: Configure which sensor classes are aggregated and how threshold helpers are created.

data.aggregates_min_entities: Minimum number of entities of the same device class required to create aggregates.
data.aggregates_binary_sensor_device_classes: Binary sensor device classes to be aggregated.
data.aggregates_sensor_device_classes: Sensor device classes to be aggregated.

data_description.aggregates_illuminance_threshold: Magic Areas will create a `light` binary sensor that will track when the area's aggregate illuminance goes over this threshold. Requires the device class `illuminance` to be selected for aggregation. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.aggregates_illuminance_threshold_hystere: [Hysteresis](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/threshold/#hysteresis) defines how sensitive the sensor is to changes. This value is a percentage of the illuminance threshold, not lux. Example: threshold `1000` with hysteresis `5` means a `50 lux` margin. A value of `0` flips whenever the threshold is crossed.

feature_conf_fan_groups.title: Fan automation
feature_conf_fan_groups.description: Choose what to configure for area fan automation in this area.

feature_conf_fan_groups_settings.title: Fan automation settings
feature_conf_fan_groups_settings.description: Configure the state, aggregate signal, and setpoint used to control fan groups.

data_description.required_state: Area state required before fan automation can turn on the fan group.
data_description.tracked_device_class: Select which aggregate device class to track for value changes.
data_description.setpoint: Minimum value the tracked aggregate sensor must reach before fan automation turns on the fan group.

feature_conf_climate_control.title: Climate automation
feature_conf_climate_control.description: Choose what to configure for climate control in this area.

feature_conf_climate_control_settings.title: Climate entity selection
feature_conf_climate_control_settings.description: Select the climate entity that should be controlled through area states.

feature_conf_climate_control_select_pres.title: Feature: Climate Control > Preset Mapping
feature_conf_climate_control_select_pres.description: Control a climate entity by mapping presets to the area state. `extended` state takes priority over `occupied` and `sleep` state takes priority over `extended` and `occupied`. Map a state to an empty preset to disable changing presets for that state.

data_description.preset_clear: Preset to be changed when on `clear` state.
data_description.preset_occupied: Preset to be changed when on `occupied` state.
data_description.preset_sleep: Preset to be changed when on `sleep` state.
data_description.preset_extended: Preset to be changed when on `extended` state.

feature_conf_ble_trackers.title: Bluetooth tracker monitoring
feature_conf_ble_trackers.description: Choose what to configure for BLE tracker presence monitoring in this area.

feature_conf_ble_trackers_settings.title: BLE tracker settings
feature_conf_ble_trackers_settings.description: Choose BLE tracker entities whose room-state changes should contribute to area occupancy.

data.ble_tracker_entities: Entities from BLE Trackers to track.

data_description.ble_tracker_entities: BLE tracker integrations such as [room-assistant](https://github.com/mKeRix/room-assistant), [Bermuda](https://github.com/agittins/bermuda), and [ESPresence](https://github.com/ESPresense/ESPresense) create sensors whose state indicates the closest area for a tracked device.

feature_conf_wasp_in_a_box.title: Wasp in a Box
feature_conf_wasp_in_a_box.description: Choose what to configure for Wasp in a Box occupancy inference in this area.

feature_conf_wasp_in_a_box_settings.title: Wasp in a Box settings
feature_conf_wasp_in_a_box_settings.description: Configure delay, timeout, and source sensor classes used by Wasp in a Box.

data_description.delay: Time to wait before checking for wasps after a box changing state. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.wasp_timeout: If greater than 0, wasp will be 'forgotten' after being inactive for this long.
data_description.wasp_device_classes: If you wish other aggregates to be considered as wasps, please select from the list.

secondary_states.title: Advanced area state tracking
secondary_states.description: You can enable [secondary states](https://github.com/jseidl/hass-magic_areas/wiki/Area-State) for a Magic Area by assigning an entity to it. Secondary states are used by features for turning lights on only when it's dark or only turning on climate devices after an `extended` period of time or not sending notifications to an area if it's on `sleep`.

data_description.dark_entity:
This entity provides the area-level `bright`/`dark` state used by Magic Areas features. Use `sun.sun` or an exterior light sensor here when you want coarse daylight-style context that is not affected by the room lights.

Light groups can use separate in-room brightness entities in `Advisory` and `Adaptive` modes. Avoid using an in-room light sensor for this area-level field unless you understand the feedback-loop risk.
data_description.sleep_entity: Entity used to track area sleep state
data_description.sleep_timeout: How long area will wait before clearing the state when on sleep state
data_description.accent_entity: Entity used to track area Accented state
data_description.extended_time: Area will be marked as 'extended' after being occupied for this long
data_description.extended_timeout: How long area will wait before clearing the state when on extended state
data_description.calculation_mode: Mode of calculating the meta-area secondary states from their children. 'Any' means if any area has the state, 'All' means all areas have to have the state and 'Majority' means >50% of the areas have to have the state.

area_config.title: Basic area options
area_config.description: Configure basic options for the area. Area type is used for the Interior/Exterior/Global [Meta-areas](https://github.com/jseidl/hass-magic_areas/wiki/Meta-Areas) and you can fine-tune which entities are analyzed by Magic Areas by using the include/exclude entities options.

data.reload_on_registry_change: Automatically reload this Magic Area on registry updates

data_description.include_entities: Magic Areas can consider entities from other areas including other Magic entities. You can use this to make a room's presence binary sensor to act as another room's presence sensor as well.
data_description.exclude_entities: Entities listed here will be completely ignored by Magic Areas. Use this if you have entities throwing your data off like device temperature sensors.
data_description.type: Defining an area type allows Meta areas for Interior/Exterior to be created.
data_description.reload_on_registry_change: Automatically reload this Magic Area when any entity or device is assigned or moved to an area.
data_description.ignore_diagnostic_entities: Magic Areas can ignore diagnostic and configuration entities which are usually not relevant.

presence_tracking.title: Presence Tracking
presence_tracking.description: Configure which platforms and binary sensor device classes are used for [presence tracking](https://github.com/jseidl/hass-magic_areas/wiki/Presence-Sensing). This section also allows you to configure how long Magic Area will wait until clearing the area after the last presence event.

data.presence_device_platforms: Platforms to be used for presence sensing

data_description.presence_device_platforms: Binary sensors are usually the standard source of presence but media devices can be used too.
data_description.presence_sensor_device_class: Define which device classes of `binary sensors` will be used for presence tracking. Common non-standard options are door and humidity.
data_description.keep_only_entities: Select presence-tracking entities that will only be considered when the room is already occupied. This is useful for preventing over-sensitive sensors such as mmWave sensors and BLE trackers false-triggering an area's presence.
data_description.clear_timeout: How long since last presence event should it wait before clearing the area

custom_control_groups.title: Custom control groups
custom_control_groups.description: Define additional Magic Areas control groups for this area. These are advanced role-style groups: choose members, the area states that should trigger the group, and the control policy that should act on it.

data_description.custom_control_groups: Each item describes one area-scoped custom group. `group_id` should be stable, `members` are the target entities, `trigger_states` are the area states that activate the group, and `policy_id` selects the control policy.

error.malformed_input: This field is invalid or required
error.climate_no_preset_support: Climate entity does not support preset modes

options.occupancy: When area goes from clear to occupied

options.inhibit: Classic: bright may block on and turn off
options.adaptive: Adaptive: bright turns off only with safeguards
